_comment: IAM Trust Policy — WebRTC API Client Role (aria-webrtc-client-role)
This trust policy allows an OIDC identity provider (Cognito User Pool,
Google, Auth0, etc.) to vend this role via AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity.
Three principal patterns are provided — use the one that matches your
identity setup and remove the others:
A) Amazon Cognito User Pool  (most common for Meridian Bank)
B) Amazon Cognito Identity Pool  (alternative — pool does the STS call)
C) Generic OIDC provider  (Google, Auth0, Okta, etc.)
Replace placeholder values before deploying:
<REGION>          e.g. eu-west-2
<ACCOUNT_ID>      12-digit AWS account ID
<USER_POOL_ID>    Cognito User Pool ID, e.g. eu-west-2_AbCdEfGhI
<APP_CLIENT_ID>   Cognito App Client ID (the 'aud' claim in the token)
<IDENTITY_POOL_ID> Cognito Identity Pool ID (for pattern B)
<OIDC_PROVIDER>   OIDC issuer URL without https://, e.g.
accounts.google.com  or  myapp.auth0.com
References:
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/id_roles_create_for-idp_oidc.html
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/STS/latest/APIReference/API_AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity.html
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cognito/latest/developerguide/role-based-access-control.html
